Title: Completion of the seven-step pathway from tabersonine to the anticancer drug precursor vindoline and its assembly in yeast
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate oxidized intermediate required for downstream 3-hydroxylation/reduction sequence leading to vindoline and vindorosine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes monooxygenation across the 2,3-double bond of tabersonine-type substrates to form unstable epoxide intermediates that are further converted by T3R to 3-hydroxy-2,3-dihydro products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of the anticancer bisindole alkaloids vinblastine and vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; vindoline pathway products are completed in specialized leaf mesophyll idioblast/laticifer cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing greatly decreases vindoline and causes strong accumulation of 16-methoxytabersonine, showing T3O is required for flux from tabersonine derivatives to vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS in Catharanthus roseus; LC-MS metabolite profiling; recombinant yeast microsome enzyme assays; substrate feeding; NMR identification of epoxide product; yeast pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D1V2 was identified from a leaf epidermis-enriched EST database and renamed T3O. 2. VIGS of T3O reduced vindoline by 89% and increased 16-methoxytabersonine about 8.5-fold. 3. Recombinant T3O converts tabersonine-type substrates to epoxide/isomeric oxidized intermediates. 4. Together with T3R, T3O enables formation of 3-hydroxy-2,3-dihydro intermediates and supports vindoline production in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially enriched about 30-fold in leaf epidermis versus whole leaf
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf epidermis-enriched in C. roseus; functional in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Catharanthus roseus cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR); T3R
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; VIGS conditions; yeast feeding with tabersonine or 16-methoxytabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Leaf epidermis EST database (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for completion of the unknown 3-hydroxylation module; bottleneck not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In the absence of sufficient T3R, unstable epoxide byproducts accumulate and reduce productive flux
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential enzyme to complete vindoline pathway engineering in microbes or plants; should be coexpressed with T3R and downstream pathway genes to avoid byproduct accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously described as CYP71D1V2 and reported inactive in amyrin-to-ursolic acid conversion; product formation is only biologically useful when coupled to T3R

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Catharanthus roseus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct 3-hydroxy-2,3-dihydro intermediate precursor for NMT and downstream vindoline/vindorosine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Reactive T3O-derived oxidized intermediate/epoxide-related products from 16-methoxytabersonine or tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the T3O-generated oxidized intermediate in the tabersonine 3-hydroxylation module to yield 2,3-dihydro-3-hydroxylated MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of the anticancer bisindole alkaloids vinblastine and vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; desacetoxyvindoline likely transported to idioblast/laticifer cells for completion to vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduces desacetoxyvindoline and causes accumulation of new 3-oxidized 16-methoxytabersonine isomers, indicating T3R channels oxidized intermediates into productive vindoline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; LC-MS metabolite profiling; recombinant protein assay; coupled enzyme assays with T3O; yeast pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADHL1 was identified from a leaf epidermis-enriched EST database and renamed T3R. 2. VIGS of T3R caused accumulation of T3O-derived 3-oxidized isomers on leaf surfaces and reduced desacetoxyvindoline by 69%. 3. T3R alone showed poor activity with isolated epoxides but efficiently formed 3-hydroxy-2,3-dihydro products when coupled with T3O-containing microsomes. 4. T3R is required with T3O to complete the 3-hydroxylation step and enable vindoline formation in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially enriched about twofold in leaf epidermis versus whole leaf
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf epidermis-enriched in C. roseus; active in reconstituted yeast pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; VIGS conditions; in vitro coupled assays; yeast substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Leaf epidermis EST database (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key partner enzyme needed to prevent accumulation of unproductive epoxide byproducts; rate-limiting status not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient T3R permits accumulation of unstable oxidized byproducts and lowers productive vindoline flux
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be coexpressed with T3O in metabolic engineering to complete productive 3-hydroxylation chemistry and avoid side products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ADHL1 purified from E. coli was not active with isolated major epoxide isomers, implying reaction depends on a short-lived intermediate generated by T3O in coupled assays

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor pool for vindoline-specific branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tabersonine at C-16 to initiate vindoline branch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of anticancer vinblastine/vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included as an established upstream pathway enzyme; together with 16OMT enables vindoline production from tabersonine in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior biochemical characterization cited; qRT-PCR tissue expression; yeast pathway assembly in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T16H2 is an early established vindoline-pathway gene. 2. Its transcripts are enriched four- to fivefold in leaf epidermis. 3. Inclusion with other six pathway genes in yeast supported vindoline formation from tabersonine.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf epidermis enriched four- to fivefold over whole leaf
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf epidermis in C. roseus; heterologously expressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential bottleneck in engineered yeast according to Discussion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May limit flux in yeast if expression/activity is insufficient
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target for boosting vindoline production; expression optimization suggested
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not newly discovered in this paper but used as part of the complete seven-gene pathway

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor 16-methoxytabersonine for T3O/T3R steps
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of anticancer vinblastine/vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the direct substrate for T3O/T3R and was required in the seven-gene yeast pathway converting tabersonine to vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior biochemical characterization cited; qRT-PCR tissue expression; yeast pathway assembly in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 16OMT is an established early vindoline-pathway gene. 2. Its transcripts are enriched four- to fivefold in leaf epidermis. 3. In yeast, inclusion of 16OMT with the full pathway enabled vindoline production from tabersonine.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf epidermis enriched four- to fivefold over whole leaf
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf epidermis in C. roseus; heterologously expressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential upstream engineering component for complete vindoline biosynthesis from tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not newly characterized here but functionally necessary for complete pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts T3O/T3R product to immediate precursor of desacetoxyvindoline
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-methylates the 3-hydroxy-2,3-dihydrotabersonine intermediate to form desacetoxyvindoline
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of anticancer vinblastine/vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; desacetoxyvindoline likely transported to idioblast/laticifer cells for final steps
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In yeast, addition of NMT after T3O/T3R enabled desacetoxyvindoline formation; discussed as a likely bottleneck limiting flux to vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR tissue expression; prior biochemical characterization cited; yeast pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NMT transcripts are enriched about threefold in leaf epidermis, revising prior assumptions about localization. 2. Addition of NMT to T3O/T3R-expressing yeast allowed desacetoxyvindoline formation. 3. NMT activity may be a bottleneck in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: About threefold higher in leaf epidermis than whole leaf
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf epidermis in C. roseus; expressed in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Possibly plastid-associated/chloroplast-associated, but unresolved in this study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Leaf epidermis EST database (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient activity leads to buildup of N-desmethyl intermediates in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Expression optimization recommended for higher microbial vindoline yields
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prior biochemical localization suggested chloroplast association; this study supports epidermal transcript enrichment

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of vindoline before acetylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates desacetoxyvindoline at C-4 en route to vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of anticancer vinblastine/vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with DAT for final vindoline production in yeast; expression is low in leaf epidermis, supporting late-step localization outside epidermis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR tissue expression; prior biochemical characterization cited; yeast pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D4H is one of the last two vindoline-pathway genes. 2. It is poorly represented in leaf epidermis relative to whole leaf. 3. Coexpression with upstream genes and DAT in yeast enables vindoline formation. 4. D4H activity may be a bottleneck in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf epidermis/whole leaf ratio 0.5 or less; associated with specialized leaf mesophyll idioblast/laticifer cells
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient expression/activity lowers final vindoline yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Optimization of D4H is recommended to improve microbial vindoline production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not newly discovered here; used to confirm completion of full pathway

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target product vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of anticancer vinblastine/vincristine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Final enzyme required for vindoline formation in yeast; low epidermal expression supports late biosynthesis in mesophyll idioblast/laticifer cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR tissue expression; prior biochemical characterization cited; yeast pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAT catalyzes the terminal step to vindoline. 2. DAT transcripts are low in leaf epidermis compared with whole leaf. 3. Coexpression with D4H and upstream genes in yeast completes vindoline production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf epidermis/whole leaf ratio 0.5 or less; associated with specialized leaf mesophyll idioblast/laticifer cells
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for vindoline production; not explicitly claimed as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal step for microbial or plant vindoline engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not newly characterized here; included in complete pathway reconstruction
